---
title: "Zebrafish scRNA-seq Annotation Review"
subtitle: "Cluster markers and zebrafish marker catalog overlap"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    page-layout: full
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
.warning-list {
  border-left: 4px solid #c2410c;
  background: #fff7ed;
  padding: 0.85rem 1rem;
  margin: 0.7rem 0 1rem;
}
.note-box {
  border-left: 4px solid #2563eb;
  background: #eff6ff;
  padding: 0.85rem 1rem;
  margin: 0.7rem 0 1rem;
}
.catalog-note {
  border-left: 4px solid #7c3aed;
  background: #f5f3ff;
  padding: 0.85rem 1rem;
  margin: 0.7rem 0 1rem;
}
.muted {
  color: #64748b;
  font-size: 0.94rem;
}
main.content {
  max-width: none;
}
.cell-output-display {
  overflow-x: auto;
}
.cell-output-display img,
.cell-output-display svg {
  max-width: 100%;
  height: auto;
}
.dataTables_wrapper {
  max-width: none;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable {
  width: auto !important;
  table-layout: auto !important;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th,
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable td {
  white-space: nowrap;
  vertical-align: top;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable td.dt-compact-number,
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-compact-number {
  min-width: 2.8rem;
  max-width: 4.8rem;
  text-align: right;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-compact-number input,
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-compact-number select {
  width: 2.9rem !important;
  min-width: 0 !important;
  max-width: 2.9rem !important;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable td.dt-short-text,
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-short-text {
  min-width: 4rem;
  max-width: 8rem;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-short-text input,
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-short-text select {
  width: 5.2rem !important;
  min-width: 0 !important;
  max-width: 5.2rem !important;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-medium-text input,
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-medium-text select {
  width: 8rem !important;
  min-width: 0 !important;
  max-width: 8rem !important;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-long-text input,
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable th.dt-long-text select {
  width: 11rem !important;
  min-width: 0 !important;
  max-width: 11rem !important;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable td.dt-long-text {
  white-space: normal;
  min-width: 9rem;
  max-width: 18rem;
  overflow-wrap: anywhere;
  word-break: normal;
  line-height: 1.25;
}
.dataTables_wrapper table.dataTable td.dt-medium-text {
  white-space: normal;
  min-width: 8rem;
  max-width: 14rem;
  overflow-wrap: anywhere;
  line-height: 1.25;
}
.status-strip {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 0.55rem;
  margin: 0.5rem 0 1rem;
}
.status-pill {
  border: 1px solid #dbe4ef;
  background: #f8fafc;
  border-radius: 999px;
  padding: 0.35rem 0.7rem;
  font-size: 0.94rem;
}
.status-pill strong {
  color: #0f172a;
}
.cluster-tabs {
  margin-top: 0.8rem;
}
.cluster-tabs details {
  border: 1px solid #dbe4ef;
  border-radius: 6px;
  margin: 0.45rem 0;
  background: #fff;
}
.cluster-tabs summary {
  cursor: pointer;
  padding: 0.65rem 0.8rem;
  font-weight: 600;
}
.cluster-tabs .cluster-tab-body {
  border-top: 1px solid #e5e7eb;
  padding: 0.7rem 0.8rem 0.9rem;
}
.candidate-table {
  width: auto;
  max-width: 100%;
  border-collapse: collapse;
  font-size: 0.92rem;
}
.candidate-table th,
.candidate-table td {
  border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb;
  padding: 0.35rem 0.55rem;
  vertical-align: top;
  white-space: nowrap;
}
.candidate-table td.long {
  white-space: normal;
  max-width: 32rem;
}
.candidate-table td.compact {
  max-width: 5rem;
  text-align: right;
}
.candidate-table td.medium {
  white-space: normal;
  max-width: 16rem;
}
.candidate-table th {
  background: #f8fafc;
}
</style>
```

```{python}
from html import escape
from pathlib import Path
import json
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import HTML, display

try:
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
except ImportError:
    plt = None

try:
    from scipy import sparse
except ImportError:
    sparse = None

try:
    import plotly.express as px
except ImportError:
    px = None

try:
    import anndata as ad
except ImportError:
    ad = None

try:
    from itables import show
    import itables.options as itable_options
    itable_options.showIndex = False
    itable_options.classes = "display compact stripe hover"
    itable_options.layout = {
        "topStart": "pageLength",
        "topEnd": "search",
        "bottomStart": "info",
        "bottomEnd": "paging",
    }
    itable_options.maxBytes = "1MB"
except ImportError:
    show = None

results_dir = Path(".")
payload = json.loads((results_dir / "annotation_result.json").read_text())
markers = pd.read_csv(results_dir / "tables" / "differential_markers.csv")
matches = pd.read_csv(results_dir / "tables" / "catalog_matches.csv")
summary = pd.read_csv(results_dir / "tables" / "cluster_annotation_summary.csv")
status = payload.get("status", {})
run_input = payload.get("input", {})
catalog = payload.get("catalog", {})
warnings = status.get("warnings", [])
catalog_id = str(catalog.get("id") or "")
catalog_path = str(catalog.get("path") or "")
catalog_label = catalog_id or Path(catalog_path).name
is_zcl_catalog = "zcl_2_marker_list" in catalog_id or "zcl_2_marker_list" in catalog_path
is_builtin_catalog = "default_catalogs" in catalog_path
methods = payload.get("methods", [])
catalog_method = next((method for method in methods if "catalog" in str(method.get("step", "")).lower()), {})
catalog_params = catalog_method.get("parameters", {})
UMAP_HEIGHT = 650
UMAP_WIDTH = 900
UMAP_FACET_PANEL_HEIGHT = 360
STATIC_PALETTE = [
    "#2563eb", "#dc2626", "#16a34a", "#9333ea", "#ea580c", "#0891b2",
    "#be123c", "#4f46e5", "#65a30d", "#c026d3", "#0f766e", "#a16207",
    "#475569", "#db2777", "#0284c7", "#7c3aed",
]

def clip_text(value, limit=110):
    text = "" if pd.isna(value) else str(value)
    return text if len(text) <= limit else text[: limit - 1] + "..."

def display_static_table(df):
    display(HTML(df.reset_index(drop=True).to_html(index=False, escape=False)))

def display_table(
    df,
    *,
    caption=None,
    page_length=10,
    order=None,
    long_text_columns=None,
    medium_text_columns=None,
    compact_columns=None,
    short_text_columns=None,
    scroll_x=True,
):
    df = df.reset_index(drop=True)
    if show is None:
        display_static_table(df)
        return
    long_text_columns = set(long_text_columns or [])
    medium_text_columns = set(medium_text_columns or [])
    compact_columns = set(compact_columns or [])
    short_text_columns = set(short_text_columns or [])
    compact_name_hints = {
        "cluster_id", "rank", "n_cells", "n_candidates", "n_matched", "n_genes",
        "n_marker_genes", "n_catalog_genes", "top_score", "score", "pval", "pval_adj",
        "log2fc", "pct_in_cluster", "pct_out_cluster",
    }
    compact_columns |= {column for column in df.columns if column in compact_name_hints}
    for column in df.columns:
        series = df[column].dropna().astype(str)
        if len(series) and series.map(len).max() <= 8 and column not in long_text_columns and column not in medium_text_columns:
            compact_columns.add(column)
    column_defs = []
    compact_targets = [index for index, column in enumerate(df.columns) if column in compact_columns]
    short_targets = [index for index, column in enumerate(df.columns) if column in short_text_columns]
    long_targets = [index for index, column in enumerate(df.columns) if column in long_text_columns]
    medium_targets = [index for index, column in enumerate(df.columns) if column in medium_text_columns]
    if compact_targets:
        column_defs.append({"targets": compact_targets, "className": "dt-compact-number", "width": "4.8rem"})
    if short_targets:
        column_defs.append({"targets": short_targets, "className": "dt-short-text", "width": "8rem"})
    if long_targets:
        column_defs.append({"targets": long_targets, "className": "dt-long-text", "width": "18rem"})
    if medium_targets:
        column_defs.append({"targets": medium_targets, "className": "dt-medium-text", "width": "14rem"})
    show(
        df,
        caption=caption,
        showIndex=False,
        scrollX=scroll_x,
        column_filters="header",
        pageLength=page_length,
        lengthMenu=[10, 25, 50, 100],
        order=order or [],
        autoWidth=True,
        autoWidthMode="fit",
        columnDefs=column_defs,
        deferRender=True,
        classes="display compact stripe hover",
    )

def plot_unavailable(reason):
    display(HTML(f"<div class='note-box'>{escape(reason)}</div>"))

def static_plot_ready():
    if plt is None:
        plot_unavailable("Matplotlib is not available, so this static figure was not rendered.")
        return False
    return True

def finish_static_plot(fig):
    fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
    for ax in fig.axes:
        ax.grid(False)
    fig.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

def polish_umap(fig, *, height=UMAP_HEIGHT, width=UMAP_WIDTH, legend_title=None, fixed_single_panel=False):
    fig.update_traces(marker={"size": 3, "opacity": 0.72})
    fig.update_yaxes(scaleanchor="x", scaleratio=1)
    fig.update_layout(
        width=width,
        height=height,
        margin={"l": 35, "r": 25, "t": 70, "b": 45},
        legend_title_text=legend_title,
    )
    if fixed_single_panel:
        fig.update_xaxes(domain=[0.0, 0.68])
        fig.update_layout(
            legend={
                "x": 0.72,
                "xanchor": "left",
                "y": 1.0,
                "yanchor": "top",
                "itemsizing": "constant",
            },
            margin={"l": 35, "r": 10, "t": 70, "b": 45},
        )
    return fig

def artifact_path(name):
    artifacts = payload.get("artifacts", {})
    value = artifacts.get(name, "")
    if str(value).startswith("results/"):
        return str(value).replace("results/", "", 1)
    return str(value)

def cluster_sort_key(value):
    text = str(value)
    return (0, int(text)) if text.isdigit() else (1, text)

def sorted_cluster_values(values):
    return sorted([str(value) for value in values], key=cluster_sort_key)

def ordered_label_values(values):
    cleaned = [str(value) for value in values if str(value) and str(value) != "nan"]
    if "no catalog match" in cleaned:
        cleaned = [value for value in cleaned if value != "no catalog match"] + ["no catalog match"]
    return cleaned

def infer_treatment(sample):
    text = str(sample).lower()
    if "control" in text or text.startswith("ctrl") or "_ctrl" in text:
        return "Control"
    if "cut" in text:
        return "Cut"
    return None

def infer_genotype(sample):
    text = str(sample).lower()
    if "wt" in text or "wildtype" in text or "wild_type" in text:
        return "WT"
    if "ko" in text or "knockout" in text or "mut" in text:
        return "KO"
    return None

def palette_for(values):
    return {value: STATIC_PALETTE[i % len(STATIC_PALETTE)] for i, value in enumerate(values)}

def parse_gene_list(value):
    if pd.isna(value) or not str(value).strip():
        return []
    return [gene.strip() for gene in str(value).split(",") if gene.strip()]

def selected_dotplot_genes(summary_df, matches_df, markers_df, max_genes=36):
    genes = []
    if len(matches_df):
        top_matches = matches_df.sort_values(["cluster_id", "rank"]).groupby("cluster_id").head(1)
        for value in top_matches.get("matched_genes", []):
            genes.extend(parse_gene_list(value)[:2])
    if len(markers_df):
        top_markers = markers_df.sort_values(["cluster_id", "rank"]).groupby("cluster_id").head(1)
        genes.extend([str(gene) for gene in top_markers.get("gene", []) if str(gene)])
    seen = set()
    ordered = []
    for gene in genes:
        if gene not in seen:
            seen.add(gene)
            ordered.append(gene)
        if len(ordered) >= max_genes:
            break
    return ordered

def load_marker_expression_summary(genes):
    if ad is None:
        return pd.DataFrame(), "anndata is not available, so marker expression was not summarized."
    if sparse is None:
        return pd.DataFrame(), "scipy.sparse is not available, so marker expression was not summarized."
    h5ad = run_input.get("h5ad")
    if not h5ad:
        return pd.DataFrame(), "No input h5ad path was recorded."
    cluster_key = run_input.get("cluster_key") or "leiden"
    layer = run_input.get("expression_layer") or "X"
    try:
        adata = ad.read_h5ad(h5ad, backed="r")
    except Exception as exc:
        return pd.DataFrame(), f"Could not read input h5ad for marker expression: {exc}"
    try:
        if cluster_key not in adata.obs:
            return pd.DataFrame(), f"Cluster key '{cluster_key}' was not found in adata.obs."
        present_genes = [gene for gene in genes if gene in adata.var_names]
        if not present_genes:
            return pd.DataFrame(), "None of the selected marker genes were found in adata.var_names."
        view = adata[:, present_genes]
        if layer and layer != "X":
            if layer not in view.layers:
                return pd.DataFrame(), f"Expression layer '{layer}' was not found in adata.layers."
            matrix = view.layers[layer]
        else:
            matrix = view.X
        if sparse.issparse(matrix):
            matrix = matrix.toarray()
        else:
            matrix = np.asarray(matrix)
        clusters = adata.obs[cluster_key].astype(str).values
        rows = []
        for cluster_id in sorted_cluster_values(pd.unique(clusters)):
            mask = clusters == cluster_id
            values = matrix[mask, :]
            if values.size == 0:
                continue
            for gene_index, gene in enumerate(present_genes):
                gene_values = values[:, gene_index]
                rows.append(
                    {
                        "cluster_id": cluster_id,
                        "gene": gene,
                        "mean_expression": float(np.nanmean(gene_values)),
                        "pct_expressing": float(np.nanmean(gene_values > 0) * 100),
                    }
                )
        return pd.DataFrame(rows), ""
    finally:
        try:
            adata.file.close()
        except Exception:
            pass

def candidate_details_html(matches_df, summary_df):
    if not len(matches_df):
        return "<div class='note-box'>No catalog-supported candidate labels were produced.</div>"
    summary_lookup = summary_df.assign(cluster_id=summary_df["cluster_id"].astype(str)).set_index("cluster_id").to_dict("index")
    html = ["<div class='cluster-tabs'>"]
    for cluster_id in sorted(matches_df["cluster_id"].astype(str).unique(), key=cluster_sort_key):
        cluster_matches = matches_df[matches_df["cluster_id"].astype(str) == cluster_id].copy()
        cluster_matches = cluster_matches.sort_values(["rank", "pval_adj", "score"], ascending=[True, True, False]).head(8)
        top = summary_lookup.get(cluster_id, {})
        top_label = top.get("top_label", "no catalog match")
        n_cells = top.get("n_cells", "")
        html.append(
            "<details>"
            f"<summary>Cluster {escape(cluster_id)} | {escape(str(top_label))} | {escape(str(n_cells))} cells | {len(cluster_matches)} candidates</summary>"
            "<div class='cluster-tab-body'>"
            "<table class='candidate-table'>"
            "<thead><tr>"
            "<th>rank</th><th>candidate label</th><th>confidence</th><th>FDR</th><th>score</th><th>n matched</th><th>matched genes</th>"
            "</tr></thead><tbody>"
        )
        for row in cluster_matches.itertuples(index=False):
            matched = clip_text(getattr(row, "matched_genes", ""), 150)
            html.append(
                "<tr>"
                f"<td class='compact'>{escape(str(getattr(row, 'rank', '')))}</td>"
                f"<td class='medium'>{escape(str(getattr(row, 'cell_type', '')))}</td>"
                f"<td>{escape(str(getattr(row, 'confidence_bucket', '')))}</td>"
                f"<td class='compact'>{escape(str(getattr(row, 'pval_adj', '')))}</td>"
                f"<td class='compact'>{escape(str(getattr(row, 'score', '')))}</td>"
                f"<td class='compact'>{escape(str(getattr(row, 'n_matched', '')))}</td>"
                f"<td class='long'>{escape(str(matched))}</td>"
                "</tr>"
            )
        html.append("</tbody></table></div></details>")
    html.append("</div>")
    return "".join(html)

def load_umap_table():
    if ad is None:
        return pd.DataFrame(), "anndata is not available, so UMAP figures were not rendered."
    h5ad = run_input.get("h5ad")
    if not h5ad:
        return pd.DataFrame(), "No input h5ad path was recorded."
    try:
        adata = ad.read_h5ad(h5ad, backed="r")
    except Exception as exc:
        return pd.DataFrame(), f"Could not read input h5ad for UMAP figures: {exc}"
    if "X_umap" not in adata.obsm:
        return pd.DataFrame(), "The input h5ad does not contain obsm['X_umap']."
    cluster_key = run_input.get("cluster_key") or "leiden"
    if cluster_key not in adata.obs:
        return pd.DataFrame(), f"Cluster key '{cluster_key}' was not found in adata.obs."
    coords = np.asarray(adata.obsm["X_umap"])
    obs = adata.obs[[cluster_key]].copy()
    obs["cluster_id"] = obs[cluster_key].astype(str).values
    sample_key = run_input.get("sample_key")
    if sample_key and sample_key in adata.obs:
        obs["sample"] = adata.obs[sample_key].astype(str).values
    else:
        obs["sample"] = ""
    obs["UMAP1"] = coords[:, 0]
    obs["UMAP2"] = coords[:, 1]
    label_map = summary.assign(cluster_id=summary["cluster_id"].astype(str)).set_index("cluster_id")
    obs = obs.reset_index(names="cell_id").merge(
        label_map[["top_label", "confidence_bucket", "review_status", "n_candidates"]],
        on="cluster_id",
        how="left",
    )
    obs["top_label"] = obs["top_label"].fillna("no catalog match")
    obs["confidence_bucket"] = obs["confidence_bucket"].fillna("unknown")
    obs["review_status"] = obs["review_status"].fillna("no catalog-supported candidate")
    obs["n_candidates"] = obs["n_candidates"].fillna(0).astype(int)
    return obs, ""
```

## Summary

This template is focused on zebrafish scRNA-seq annotation. It ranks cluster markers, tests those
markers against an explicit zebrafish marker catalog, and produces review-oriented tables and
figures. The catalog enrichment score is evidence for review, not a classifier probability or final
cell type assignment.

```{python}
display_static_table(
    pd.DataFrame(
        [
            {"field": "state", "value": status.get("state", "unknown")},
            {"field": "organism", "value": run_input.get("organism", "")},
            {"field": "tissue", "value": run_input.get("tissue") or "not provided"},
            {"field": "stage", "value": run_input.get("stage") or "not provided"},
            {"field": "cluster key", "value": run_input.get("cluster_key", "")},
            {"field": "expression layer", "value": run_input.get("expression_layer", "")},
            {"field": "catalog", "value": catalog_label},
            {"field": "clusters", "value": len(summary)},
            {"field": "clusters with candidate labels", "value": int((summary["top_label"].fillna("") != "no catalog match").sum()) if len(summary) else 0},
        ]
    )
)
```

```{python}
if is_zcl_catalog:
    display(HTML(
        "<div class='catalog-note'><strong>Catalog note.</strong> "
        "This run uses the ZCL 2.0 marker list converted into the template schema. "
        "This is the recommended automatic baseline for whole-fish zebrafish annotation, "
        "including 72 hpf/3 dpf-style context.</div>"
    ))
elif is_builtin_catalog:
    display(HTML(
        "<div class='catalog-note'><strong>Catalog note.</strong> "
        "This run uses the bundled starter catalog. Replace it with a project-specific "
        "curated zebrafish marker catalog before making biological claims.</div>"
    ))
```

## How to Read This Report

- Start with **Cluster Annotation Candidates** for the current best catalog-supported label per
  cluster.
- Use **Catalog Match Score** to see whether a cluster has one strong candidate or several weak
  alternatives.
- Use **Top Marker Evidence** to check whether the candidate label is supported by strong ranked
  markers.
- `score` is `-log10(FDR-adjusted enrichment p-value)`, not a probability.
- `confidence_bucket` is a heuristic review aid.
- Missing tissue or stage means the interpretation is context-light.

## Database Used

```{python}
database_rows = [
    {"field": "catalog id", "value": catalog.get("id", "")},
    {"field": "catalog type", "value": "ZCL 2.0 marker-list download" if is_zcl_catalog else "local or built-in TSV catalog"},
    {"field": "catalog species", "value": catalog.get("species", "")},
    {"field": "catalog rows", "value": catalog.get("n_rows", "")},
    {"field": "catalog sources", "value": ", ".join(catalog.get("sources", []))},
    {"field": "FDR threshold", "value": catalog_params.get("fdr_threshold", "")},
    {"field": "minimum log2FC", "value": catalog_params.get("min_log2fc", "")},
]
display_static_table(pd.DataFrame(database_rows))
```

## Download Results

```{python}
excel_path = artifact_path("excel_workbook") or "scrna_annotate_zebrafish_results.xlsx"
annotated_h5ad_path = artifact_path("annotated_h5ad") or "adata.annotated.h5ad"
if Path(excel_path).exists():
    display(HTML(
        f"<div class='note-box'><strong>Excel workbook.</strong> "
        f"<a href='{escape(excel_path)}' download>Download all annotation tables</a>. "
        "Sheets: cluster_summary, catalog_matches, differential_markers.</div>"
    ))
else:
    display(HTML("<div class='note-box'>Excel workbook was not found for this run.</div>"))
if Path(annotated_h5ad_path).exists():
    display(HTML(
        f"<div class='note-box'><strong>Annotated H5AD.</strong> "
        f"<a href='{escape(annotated_h5ad_path)}' download>Download annotated AnnData</a>. "
        "Annotation review columns are stored in adata.obs.</div>"
    ))
else:
    display(HTML("<div class='note-box'>Annotated H5AD was not found for this run.</div>"))
```

```{python}
if is_zcl_catalog:
    display(HTML(
        "<div class='catalog-note'><strong>Database interpretation.</strong> "
        "ZCL 2.0 markers are single-cell landscape markers. They are appropriate as an automatic "
        "baseline for whole-fish data, but final labels should still be reviewed against marker genes, "
        "stage, tissue context, and any FishSCT or literature-derived project markers.</div>"
    ))
```

## Warnings

```{python}
if warnings:
    html = "<div class='warning-list'>" + "".join(
        f"<p><strong>Warning {i}.</strong> {escape(str(warning))}</p>"
        for i, warning in enumerate(warnings, start=1)
    ) + "</div>"
else:
    html = "<div class='note-box'>No warnings were recorded.</div>"
display(HTML(html))
```

## Cluster Annotation Candidates

This table is the main review table. `no catalog match` means the catalog did not produce a
candidate for that cluster under the current enrichment threshold. The full candidate evidence is
kept in **Catalog Matches** and in the downloadable Excel workbook.

```{python}
summary_columns = ["cluster_id", "n_cells", "top_label", "confidence_bucket", "top_score", "matched_genes"]
summary_table = summary[[column for column in summary_columns if column in summary]].copy()
summary_table = summary_table.fillna("")
if "top_score" in summary_table:
    summary_table["top_score"] = pd.to_numeric(summary_table["top_score"], errors="coerce").round(3)
for column in ["matched_genes"]:
    if column in summary_table:
        summary_table[column] = summary_table[column].map(lambda value: clip_text(value, 80))
summary_table = summary_table.rename(columns={"matched_genes": "matched gene evidence"})
display_table(
    summary_table,
    caption="Cluster annotation summary",
    page_length=10,
    order=[[0, "asc"]],
    long_text_columns=["matched gene evidence"],
    medium_text_columns=["top_label"],
    scroll_x=False,
)
```

### Review Overview

```{python}
status_counts = summary["review_status"].fillna("unknown").value_counts().reset_index()
status_counts.columns = ["review_status", "n_clusters"]
candidate_counts = summary["n_candidates"].fillna(0).astype(int).value_counts().sort_index().reset_index()
candidate_counts.columns = ["n_candidates", "n_clusters"]
pill_html = "<div class='status-strip'>" + "".join(
    f"<span class='status-pill'><strong>{escape(str(row.n_clusters))}</strong> {escape(str(row.review_status))}</span>"
    for row in status_counts.itertuples(index=False)
) + "</div>"
display(HTML(pill_html))
```

### Candidate Details by Cluster

Cluster IDs are kept exactly as produced by Scanpy/Leiden in `adata.obs`; for this dataset they are
0-based (`0` to `31`). Keeping the original IDs avoids mismatch with UMAP, CSV/Excel outputs, and
downstream project files.

```{python}
display(HTML(candidate_details_html(matches, summary)))
```

### Match Diagnostics

```{python}
diagnostics = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"metric": "clusters total", "value": len(summary)},
        {"metric": "clusters with catalog candidates", "value": int((summary["top_label"].fillna("") != "no catalog match").sum()) if len(summary) else 0},
        {"metric": "clusters without catalog candidates", "value": int((summary["top_label"].fillna("") == "no catalog match").sum()) if len(summary) else 0},
        {"metric": "catalog match rows", "value": len(matches)},
        {"metric": "ranked marker rows", "value": len(markers)},
    ]
)
display_static_table(diagnostics)
```

```{python}
unmatched = summary.loc[summary["top_label"].fillna("") == "no catalog match", ["cluster_id", "n_cells"]].copy()
if len(unmatched):
    top_unmatched_markers = (
        markers[markers["cluster_id"].astype(str).isin(unmatched["cluster_id"].astype(str))]
        .sort_values(["cluster_id", "rank"])
        .groupby("cluster_id")
        .head(8)
        .groupby("cluster_id")["gene"]
        .apply(lambda values: ", ".join(map(str, values)))
        .reset_index(name="top ranked markers")
    )
    unmatched = unmatched.merge(top_unmatched_markers, on="cluster_id", how="left")
    display_table(
        unmatched,
        caption="Clusters without catalog-supported candidates",
        page_length=10,
        order=[[0, "asc"]],
        long_text_columns=["top ranked markers"],
    )
else:
    display(HTML("<div class='note-box'>All clusters have at least one catalog-supported candidate label.</div>"))
```

## Figures

### UMAP Overview

```{python}
umap, umap_message = load_umap_table()
if px is None:
    plot_unavailable("Plotly is not available, so UMAP figures were not rendered.")
elif len(umap):
    fig = px.scatter(
        umap,
        x="UMAP1",
        y="UMAP2",
        color="cluster_id",
        hover_data=["cell_id", "cluster_id", "top_label", "confidence_bucket", "sample"],
        title="UMAP by cluster",
        template="plotly_white",
        render_mode="webgl",
    )
    polish_umap(fig, legend_title="Cluster", fixed_single_panel=True).show()

    fig = px.scatter(
        umap,
        x="UMAP1",
        y="UMAP2",
        color="top_label",
        hover_data=["cell_id", "cluster_id", "top_label", "confidence_bucket", "review_status", "sample"],
        title="UMAP by top catalog-supported label",
        template="plotly_white",
        render_mode="webgl",
    )
    polish_umap(fig, legend_title="Top label", fixed_single_panel=True).show()

    if "sample" in umap and (umap["sample"].astype(str) != "").any():
        sample_count = int(umap["sample"].astype(str).nunique())
        facet_height = max(UMAP_HEIGHT, UMAP_FACET_PANEL_HEIGHT * int(np.ceil(sample_count / 2)))
        fig = px.scatter(
            umap,
            x="UMAP1",
            y="UMAP2",
            color="top_label",
            facet_col="sample",
            facet_col_wrap=2,
            hover_data=["cell_id", "sample", "cluster_id", "top_label", "confidence_bucket"],
            title="UMAP by sample, colored by top catalog-supported label",
            template="plotly_white",
            render_mode="webgl",
        )
        fig.for_each_annotation(lambda annotation: annotation.update(text=annotation.text.replace("sample=", "")))
        polish_umap(fig, height=facet_height, legend_title="Top label").show()

        design_umap = umap.copy()
        design_umap["treatment"] = design_umap["sample"].map(infer_treatment)
        design_umap["genotype"] = design_umap["sample"].map(infer_genotype)
        design_umap = design_umap.dropna(subset=["treatment", "genotype"])
        if len(design_umap):
            design_umap["treatment"] = pd.Categorical(design_umap["treatment"], categories=["Control", "Cut"], ordered=True)
            design_umap["genotype"] = pd.Categorical(design_umap["genotype"], categories=["WT", "KO"], ordered=True)
            fig = px.scatter(
                design_umap,
                x="UMAP1",
                y="UMAP2",
                color="top_label",
                facet_col="treatment",
                facet_row="genotype",
                category_orders={"treatment": ["Control", "Cut"], "genotype": ["WT", "KO"]},
                hover_data=["cell_id", "sample", "genotype", "treatment", "cluster_id", "top_label", "confidence_bucket"],
                title="UMAP by genotype and treatment, colored by top catalog-supported label",
                template="plotly_white",
                render_mode="webgl",
            )
            fig.for_each_annotation(
                lambda annotation: annotation.update(
                    text=annotation.text.replace("treatment=", "").replace("genotype=", "")
                )
            )
            polish_umap(fig, height=760, legend_title="Top label").show()
        else:
            plot_unavailable("Treatment/genotype UMAP was not rendered because Control/Cut and WT/KO could not be inferred from sample names.")
else:
    plot_unavailable(umap_message or "UMAP figures were not available.")
```

### Sample Composition

This static composition plot is intended for quick sample and condition review. It shows whether
candidate annotations are broadly represented across samples.

```{python}
if not static_plot_ready():
    pass
elif not len(umap):
    plot_unavailable(umap_message or "UMAP data were not available for sample composition plots.")
elif "sample" not in umap or not (umap["sample"].astype(str) != "").any():
    plot_unavailable("No sample column was available for sample composition plots.")
else:
    composition = umap.copy()
    composition["sample"] = composition["sample"].astype(str)
    composition["top_label"] = composition["top_label"].fillna("no catalog match").astype(str)
    label_order = ordered_label_values(composition["top_label"].value_counts().index)
    sample_order = list(composition["sample"].value_counts().sort_index().index)
    label_counts = composition.groupby(["sample", "top_label"]).size().unstack(fill_value=0).reindex(sample_order)
    label_counts = label_counts.reindex(label_order, axis=1, fill_value=0)
    label_props = label_counts.div(label_counts.sum(axis=1), axis=0).fillna(0)
    colors = palette_for(label_props.columns)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10.2, max(4.2, 0.48 * len(label_props.index) + 2.2)))
    left = np.zeros(len(label_props.index))
    y = np.arange(len(label_props.index))
    for label in label_props.columns:
        values = label_props[label].values
        ax.barh(y, values, left=left, color=colors[label], label=label, height=0.72)
        left += values
    ax.set_yticks(y)
    ax.set_yticklabels(label_props.index)
    ax.invert_yaxis()
    ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
    ax.set_xlabel("Fraction of cells")
    ax.set_title("Candidate label composition by sample")
    ax.legend(loc="center left", bbox_to_anchor=(1.01, 0.5), frameon=False, title="Top label")
    finish_static_plot(fig)
```

### Candidate Ranking

This static bubble plot shows the top three catalog-supported candidate tiers per cluster. Color is
the within-cluster candidate tier: rank 1, 2, or 3. Candidate rows are sorted by the cluster where
they have their strongest support, so the plot reads from cluster 0 toward the final cluster.

```{python}
if not static_plot_ready():
    pass
elif not len(matches):
    plot_unavailable("No catalog-supported candidates were available for the candidate ranking plot.")
else:
    candidate_plot = matches.copy()
    candidate_plot["cluster_id"] = candidate_plot["cluster_id"].astype(str)
    candidate_plot["cell_type"] = candidate_plot["cell_type"].astype(str)
    candidate_plot["score_num"] = pd.to_numeric(candidate_plot["score"], errors="coerce").fillna(0)
    candidate_plot["pval_adj_num"] = pd.to_numeric(candidate_plot["pval_adj"], errors="coerce").fillna(1)
    candidate_plot = candidate_plot.sort_values(["cluster_id", "pval_adj_num", "score_num", "cell_type"], ascending=[True, True, False, True])
    ranked_parts = []
    for cluster_id, cluster_df in candidate_plot.groupby("cluster_id", sort=False):
        cluster_df = cluster_df.copy()
        score_key = cluster_df["score_num"].round(10)
        pval_key = cluster_df["pval_adj_num"].round(12)
        tier_key = list(zip(pval_key, score_key))
        tier_lookup = {}
        display_ranks = []
        for key in tier_key:
            if key not in tier_lookup:
                tier_lookup[key] = len(tier_lookup) + 1
            display_ranks.append(tier_lookup[key])
        cluster_df["display_rank"] = display_ranks
        ranked_parts.append(cluster_df)
    candidate_plot = pd.concat(ranked_parts, ignore_index=True)
    candidate_plot = candidate_plot[candidate_plot["display_rank"] <= 3].copy()
    row_stats = (
        candidate_plot.sort_values(["cell_type", "display_rank", "pval_adj_num", "score_num"], ascending=[True, True, True, False])
        .groupby("cell_type")
        .agg(best_cluster=("cluster_id", "first"), best_rank=("display_rank", "min"), best_score=("score_num", "max"))
        .reset_index()
    )
    row_stats["best_cluster_sort"] = row_stats["best_cluster"].map(cluster_sort_key)
    label_order = row_stats.sort_values(["best_cluster_sort", "best_rank", "best_score", "cell_type"], ascending=[True, True, False, True])["cell_type"].tolist()
    cluster_order = sorted_cluster_values(candidate_plot["cluster_id"].unique())
    x_lookup = {cluster_id: i for i, cluster_id in enumerate(cluster_order)}
    y_lookup = {label: i for i, label in enumerate(label_order)}
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(min(10.2, max(7.8, 0.22 * len(cluster_order) + 4.8)), max(5.5, 0.22 * len(label_order) + 2.2)))
    rank_colors = {1: "#2563eb", 2: "#f59e0b", 3: "#94a3b8"}
    for display_rank in [3, 2, 1]:
        subset = candidate_plot[candidate_plot["display_rank"] == display_rank]
        if not len(subset):
            continue
        sizes = 18 + 7 * subset["score_num"].clip(lower=0, upper=12)
        ax.scatter(
            subset["cluster_id"].map(x_lookup),
            subset["cell_type"].map(y_lookup),
            s=sizes,
            color=rank_colors[display_rank],
            alpha=0.82,
            edgecolor="white",
            linewidth=0.35,
            label=f"Rank {display_rank}",
        )
    ax.set_xticks(range(len(cluster_order)))
    ax.set_xticklabels(cluster_order)
    ax.set_yticks(range(len(label_order)))
    ax.set_yticklabels(label_order)
    ax.invert_yaxis()
    ax.set_xlabel("Cluster")
    ax.set_ylabel("Catalog candidate label")
    ax.set_title("Top catalog candidate tiers per cluster")
    ax.legend(loc="center left", bbox_to_anchor=(1.01, 0.5), frameon=False, title="Candidate tier")
    finish_static_plot(fig)
```

### Marker Expression Dot Plot

The dot plot summarizes a compact set of marker genes selected from top catalog matches and top
ranked cluster markers. Dot size is the percentage of cells expressing the gene; color is mean
expression in the configured expression layer.

```{python}
if not static_plot_ready():
    pass
else:
    selected_genes = selected_dotplot_genes(summary, matches, markers)
    expression_summary, expression_message = load_marker_expression_summary(selected_genes)
    if not len(expression_summary):
        plot_unavailable(expression_message or "No marker expression summary was available.")
    else:
        expression_summary["cluster_id"] = expression_summary["cluster_id"].astype(str)
        expression_summary["gene"] = expression_summary["gene"].astype(str)
        cluster_order = sorted_cluster_values(expression_summary["cluster_id"].unique())
        gene_order = list(dict.fromkeys(selected_genes))
        gene_order = [gene for gene in gene_order if gene in set(expression_summary["gene"])]
        x_lookup = {cluster_id: i for i, cluster_id in enumerate(cluster_order)}
        y_lookup = {gene: i for i, gene in enumerate(gene_order)}
        expression_summary = expression_summary[expression_summary["gene"].isin(gene_order)].copy()
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(min(10.2, max(7.8, 0.22 * len(cluster_order) + 4.8)), max(5.5, 0.22 * len(gene_order) + 2.4)))
        sizes = 10 + 1.65 * expression_summary["pct_expressing"].clip(lower=0, upper=100)
        scatter = ax.scatter(
            expression_summary["cluster_id"].map(x_lookup),
            expression_summary["gene"].map(y_lookup),
            s=sizes,
            c=expression_summary["mean_expression"],
            cmap="magma",
            alpha=0.82,
            edgecolor="white",
            linewidth=0.35,
        )
        ax.set_xticks(range(len(cluster_order)))
        ax.set_xticklabels(cluster_order)
        ax.set_yticks(range(len(gene_order)))
        ax.set_yticklabels(gene_order)
        ax.invert_yaxis()
        ax.set_xlabel("Cluster")
        ax.set_ylabel("Marker gene")
        ax.set_title("Selected marker expression by cluster")
        cbar = fig.colorbar(scatter, ax=ax, pad=0.01)
        cbar.set_label("Mean expression")
        size_handles = [
            ax.scatter([], [], s=10 + 1.65 * pct, color="#94a3b8", alpha=0.75, edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.35, label=f"{pct}%")
            for pct in [25, 50, 75]
        ]
        ax.legend(handles=size_handles, title="% expressing", loc="center left", bbox_to_anchor=(1.12, 0.5), frameon=False)
        finish_static_plot(fig)
```

### Catalog Match Score

Higher scores indicate stronger FDR-controlled enrichment between informative cluster markers and a catalog label.
Rows with broadly expressed genes or anatomy-level labels should be interpreted conservatively.

```{python}
if px is None:
    plot_unavailable("Plotly is not available, so the catalog score figure was not rendered.")
elif len(matches):
    top = matches.query("rank <= 5").copy()
    matrix = top.pivot_table(index="cell_type", columns="cluster_id", values="score", aggfunc="max", fill_value=0)
    row_stats = pd.DataFrame(
        {
            "cell_type": matrix.index.astype(str),
            "best_cluster": matrix.idxmax(axis=1).astype(str).values,
            "best_score": matrix.max(axis=1).values,
        }
    )
    row_stats["best_cluster_sort"] = row_stats["best_cluster"].map(cluster_sort_key)
    row_order = (
        row_stats
        .sort_values(["best_cluster_sort", "best_score", "cell_type"], ascending=[True, False, True])
        ["cell_type"]
    )
    matrix = matrix.loc[row_order]
    matrix = matrix.reindex(sorted(matrix.columns, key=lambda value: cluster_sort_key(str(value))), axis=1)
    fig = px.imshow(
        matrix,
        aspect="auto",
        color_continuous_scale="Viridis",
        labels={"x": "Cluster", "y": "Catalog cell type", "color": "Score"},
        title="Zebrafish marker catalog enrichment score",
        template="plotly_white",
    )
    fig.update_layout(height=max(420, 28 * len(matrix.index)), margin={"l": 130, "r": 30, "t": 70, "b": 50})
    fig.show()
else:
    plot_unavailable("No catalog matches were produced.")
```

### Top Marker Evidence

This figure shows the strongest few ranked markers per cluster. It is intentionally compact; use the
differential marker table for exhaustive review.

```{python}
if px is None:
    plot_unavailable("Plotly is not available, so the top-marker figure was not rendered.")
elif len(markers):
    plot_markers = markers.copy()
    plot_markers["pval_adj_num"] = pd.to_numeric(plot_markers["pval_adj"], errors="coerce")
    plot_markers["log2fc_num"] = pd.to_numeric(plot_markers["log2fc"], errors="coerce")
    plot_markers = plot_markers.sort_values(["cluster_id", "rank"]).groupby("cluster_id").head(5)
    plot_markers = plot_markers.sort_values(["cluster_id", "rank"]).reset_index(drop=True)
    plot_markers["neg_log10_padj"] = plot_markers["pval_adj_num"].clip(lower=1e-300).map(lambda value: -np.log10(value) if pd.notna(value) else None)
    plot_markers["cluster_marker"] = plot_markers.apply(lambda row: f"C{row['cluster_id']} | {row['gene']}", axis=1)
    plot_markers["cluster_marker"] = pd.Categorical(plot_markers["cluster_marker"], categories=list(reversed(plot_markers["cluster_marker"])), ordered=True)
    fig = px.scatter(
        plot_markers,
        x="log2fc_num",
        y="cluster_marker",
        size=plot_markers["log2fc_num"].clip(lower=0),
        color="neg_log10_padj",
        hover_data=["cluster_id", "gene", "rank", "score", "log2fc", "pval_adj", "strength"],
        labels={"log2fc_num": "log2 fold change", "cluster_marker": "Cluster | marker gene", "color": "-log10 adjusted p-value"},
        title="Top marker evidence per cluster",
        template="plotly_white",
    )
    fig.update_layout(height=min(760, max(420, 24 * len(plot_markers) + 120)), margin={"l": 155, "r": 30, "t": 70, "b": 50})
    fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=False)
    fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=False)
    fig.show()
else:
    plot_unavailable("No differential markers were produced.")
```

### Full Marker Evidence

This interactive figure shows all retained marker rows. Gene labels are hidden on the axis to keep
the plot compact; hover over points to inspect the cluster, gene, rank, log2FC, and adjusted p-value.

```{python}
if px is None:
    plot_unavailable("Plotly is not available, so the full-marker figure was not rendered.")
elif len(markers):
    full_markers = markers.copy()
    full_markers["pval_adj_num"] = pd.to_numeric(full_markers["pval_adj"], errors="coerce")
    full_markers["log2fc_num"] = pd.to_numeric(full_markers["log2fc"], errors="coerce")
    full_markers = full_markers.sort_values(["cluster_id", "rank"]).reset_index(drop=True)
    full_markers["neg_log10_padj"] = full_markers["pval_adj_num"].clip(lower=1e-300).map(lambda value: -np.log10(value) if pd.notna(value) else None)
    full_markers["marker_row"] = full_markers.apply(lambda row: f"C{row['cluster_id']} r{int(row['rank'])} | {row['gene']}", axis=1)
    full_markers["marker_row"] = pd.Categorical(full_markers["marker_row"], categories=list(reversed(full_markers["marker_row"])), ordered=True)
    fig = px.scatter(
        full_markers,
        x="log2fc_num",
        y="marker_row",
        size=full_markers["log2fc_num"].clip(lower=0),
        color="neg_log10_padj",
        hover_data=["cluster_id", "gene", "rank", "score", "log2fc", "pval_adj", "strength"],
        labels={"log2fc_num": "log2 fold change", "marker_row": "Marker rows", "color": "-log10 adjusted p-value"},
        title="Full marker evidence, hover for gene labels",
        template="plotly_white",
    )
    fig.update_yaxes(showticklabels=False, title="Marker rows, ordered by cluster and rank")
    fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=False)
    fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=False)
    fig.update_traces(marker={"opacity": 0.72})
    fig.update_layout(height=min(900, max(460, 0.32 * len(full_markers) + 180)), margin={"l": 40, "r": 30, "t": 70, "b": 50})
    fig.show()
else:
    plot_unavailable("No differential markers were produced.")
```

## Evidence Tables

### Catalog Matches

This preview shows the strongest catalog matches only. The full table is available in
`tables/catalog_matches.csv` and the Excel workbook.

```{python}
match_table = matches.sort_values(["cluster_id", "rank"]).groupby("cluster_id").head(3).copy()
for column in ["matched_genes", "missing_genes", "citation"]:
    if column in match_table:
        match_table[column] = match_table[column].map(lambda value: clip_text(value, 90))
display_table(
    match_table,
    caption="Zebrafish catalog matches, top 3 per cluster",
    page_length=8,
    order=[[0, "asc"], [1, "asc"]],
    long_text_columns=["matched_genes", "missing_genes", "citation"],
)
```

### Differential Markers

```{python}
marker_preview = markers.sort_values(["cluster_id", "rank"]).groupby("cluster_id").head(5).copy()
display_table(
    marker_preview,
    caption="Differential markers, top 5 per cluster",
    page_length=10,
    order=[[0, "asc"], [1, "asc"]],
)
```

## Provenance

```{python}
provenance = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"field": "input h5ad", "value": run_input.get("h5ad", "")},
        {"field": "input source template", "value": run_input.get("input_source_template") or "not provided"},
        {"field": "catalog id", "value": catalog.get("id", "")},
        {"field": "catalog path", "value": catalog.get("path", "")},
        {"field": "catalog sha256", "value": catalog.get("sha256", "")},
        {"field": "catalog species", "value": catalog.get("species", "")},
        {"field": "catalog rows", "value": catalog.get("n_rows", "")},
        {"field": "catalog sources", "value": ", ".join(catalog.get("sources", []))},
    ]
)
display_static_table(provenance)
```

## Interpretation Notes

- This report uses zebrafish catalog evidence only.
- Replace the example marker catalog before interpreting biological labels.
- Treat catalog matches as review candidates, especially when tissue or developmental stage is not provided.
- ZCL 2.0 is the default automatic baseline for whole-fish zebrafish data.
- Add FishSCT-derived project-local markers when a cluster needs more tissue or dataset-specific support.
- Compare marker genes, catalog matches, and UMAP/cluster biology before freezing final labels.

## References

- Wolf FA, Angerer P, Theis FJ. 2018. [SCANPY: large-scale single-cell gene expression data analysis](https://genomebiology.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s13059-017-1382-0). *Genome Biology*.
- Heumos L et al. [Single-cell best practices: cell type annotation](https://www.sc-best-practices.org/cellular_structure/annotation.html).
